original_question: True and False or ( not True ) is
subquestion: Can you edit a negative instance of Boolean Algebra so that it now qualifies as that concept?
judge_answer:
Yes, you can edit a negative instance of Boolean Algebra so that it now qualifies as that concept. In Boolean Algebra, a negative instance refers to a situation where the values of the variables are inverted. To make a negative instance of Boolean Algebra qualify as a positive instance, you can simply invert the values of the variables. For example, if you have a negative instance of Boolean Algebra where the values of the variables are 0 and 1, you can invert the values to make it a positive instance where the values of the variables are 1 and 0. This will make the negative instance of Boolean Algebra qualify as a positive instance.
Final Answer: correct
